findings.issue: Error handling via 'on error call GoalName' has zero PLang test coverage — the LLM builder cannot reliably generate the onError step property
findings.impact: Error handler goal invocation, error variable propagation (%__error__%, %__errorKey__%, %__errorStatusCode__%), and retry logic are all untested at the integration level
findings.suggestion: Pre-build .pr files for error handling tests (skip LLM generation) or add builder post-processing to extract onError from step text

findings.issue: list.range returns types.list wrapper but assert only checks 'is not null' — cannot verify count due to builder stripping .property from %variable% references
findings.impact: A range that returns an empty list would still pass the test
findings.suggestion: Once builder supports deep property references in assert parameters, add 'assert %rangeResult.count% equals 5'

findings.issue: list.flatten only verified as 'not null' — no count or value assertion
findings.impact: Flatten could return wrong data and test would still pass
findings.suggestion: Add count verification once builder deep-property issue is resolved

findings.issue: variable.clear test depends on variable.exists working correctly — if exists has a bug, clear test gives false results
findings.impact: Low — exists is independently tested in the same suite
findings.suggestion: Acceptable coupled testing given PLang's sequential step model

summary: All 29 PLang tests pass, all 1500 C# tests pass. Six new test suites added covering goal.call, variable ops, context variables, convert types, list operations, and math.random. Major gap remains: error handling integration tests blocked by LLM builder unreliability. Three minor findings about weak assertions in list tests due to builder limitations. Overall test quality is honest — tests verify intent where builder allows, and are documented where they can't.
